title: How come none of the characters have a southern accent
display_title: How come none of the characters have a southern accent
summary:
[]: How come none of the characters have a southern accent?             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:12 pm No fair. A lot of English dubs of other animes I've seen have at least one character with a southern accent. Why doesn't Eva have one? Hikari doesn't count. I've been around enough southerners to know that doesn't pass as southern.

[]:           &nbs

Article: How come none of the characters have a southern accent
[]: How come none of the characters have a southern accent?             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:12 pm No fair. A lot of English dubs of other animes I've seen have at least one character with a southern accent. Why doesn't Eva have one? Hikari doesn't count. I've been around enough southerners to know that doesn't pass as southern.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:25 pm Because nobody likes the South. </irony>

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:26 pm Reichu wrote: Because nobody likes the South. </irony>

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Tue Sep 26, 2006 10:28 pm Even though I don't watch Samurai Deeper Kyo, I saw the English dub, and the white-haired dude who was not Nobunaga, no. Just... Just no.

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Tue Sep 26, 2006 11:17 pm What, you mean like Touji? Would you like for them to have interpreted his Kansai dialect as a Southern drawl as is often done? Would that really have made you happy? Really ?

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Tue Sep 26, 2006 11:28 pm A Russian man. I kid you not.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Sep 27, 2006 1:08 am A better question would be why animes that take place in Japan actually have people with southern accents doing the voices (cringes at mention of abenobashi)

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Wed Sep 27, 2006 1:12 am after buying Azumanga Daiho...southern accents in anime...usually dont work at all...

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 27, 2006 1:51 pm Generally anime ADR directors try to use actors that can perform without a regional specific accent. This is important because quite often, these English dubs get viewed be people in other countries, such as Canada, the UK,

Article: How come none of the characters have a southern accent
6 1:51 pm Generally anime ADR directors try to use actors that can perform without a regional specific accent. This is important because quite often, these English dubs get viewed be people in other countries, such as Canada, the UK, Australia, etc. Sometimes, they try to translate Japanese dialects to the appropriate American accent, but it usually doesn't work so well. In Evangelion Touji has an Osaka accent, but not one of the three Touji dub actors acted with an accent to reflect that. In the manga however, Touji duz have dis sort a accent dat makes im sound like a sutherner or a back water hick.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Wed Sep 27, 2006 2:02 pm I thought it was a Brooklyn accent..?

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Wed Sep 27, 2006 2:18 pm Aye, an' Excel Saga 's Sumiyoshi hes this serta pseudo-eirish scotsmun deelect thet fer tha furst fie volyems es nerly unintel'jible.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Sep 27, 2006 2:52 pm BobBQ wrote: I thought it was a Brooklyn accent..?

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Wed Sep 27, 2006 3:42 pm Southern accents need to be kept out of anime at any cost. They make the character sound unintelligent and sometimes the accent is so bad it can make your ears bleed. (I.e. Kitsune from Love Hina. Her and Sue are some of the worst dubs you will ever hear.)

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 27, 2006 3:46 pm We could equally speculate why tha's nowt ba waa of a Northern accent, ba 'eck. (Atrocious cod-Yorkshire/Lancashire accent attempted).

[]:             Quote Post by Mundane » Wed Sep 27, 2006 3:48 pm I'm from Montgomery, Alabama and I can't understand what you just said.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 27, 2006 3:55 pm In standard orthography, rather than phonetic/dialect ... why there's

Article: How come none of the characters have a southern accent
 can't understand what you just said.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 27, 2006 3:55 pm In standard orthography, rather than phonetic/dialect ... why there's nothing by way of a Northern accent, by heck!

[]:             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Wed Sep 27, 2006 10:56 pm The idea behind using accented English to portray Japanese that was originally accented is not necessarily to suggest the one "correct" equivalent (it seems unlikely there could be such exact equivalency) as to suggest the very important point that in real life these accents do exist, and people are constantly judged by them. Okayama people really do speak differently from people in Osaka, who in turn speak differently from people in Fukuoka, who in turn speak differently from those in Tokyo--supposedly, "standard" Japanese. I personally believe that a Brooklyn accent is more culturally similar to Kansai-ben than a "Southern" accent, but again the point is not so much to say which is correct, but to acknowledge that the character has a different background. It's supposed to be at least a little jarring--that's why it's considered an "accent."

[]:             Quote Post by master0rolando » Thu Sep 28, 2006 5:15 am it should be the "fuggedaboutit" accent to replace Osakan... thats one thing i liked about Osaka better in the AzuManga than in AzuAnime

[]:             Quote Post by Evangelionfreak » Thu Sep 28, 2006 2:48 pm I would be very sad if they ruined the Evangelion dub even more by putting accents in it. they ruined the eng version of Azumanga Daioh, D.N.Angel and Spiral for me cuz they had those horrible accents.

[]:             Quote Post by KhakiBlueThunderSocks » Fri Sep 29, 2006 11:52 am The question we should be asking is why Kaworu had a lisp in the TV version, but in D&R and EoE his lisp was gone! (I know, two different voice actors, but they're RADICALLY different from each other!)
